meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Solve 'No Project Ideas'
content.seo_description: Stop guessing. Learn how to use analytics to identify profitable SaaS opportunities. A beginner-friendly guide using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' trap stems from building in a vacuum. By implementing event-based analytics into a lean MVP, you shift from guessing to observing user pain points. Use Antigravity to scaffold your tracking and Claude Code to iterate based on real-world interaction data. Stop building; start solving.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the silent killer of ambition. You’ve got the technical stack, the drive, and the sleepless nights, but you’re staring at a void where a billion-dollar product should be. Most founders think they need a 'eureka' moment, but in reality, product-market fit is found in the trenches of user behavior data.

We are moving you from the world of abstract ideation to empirical evidence. By instrumenting your MVP with surgical precision, you stop projecting your biases onto the market and start listening to what users actually struggle with. This guide gives you the blueprint to turn raw telemetry into a profitable product roadmap.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the one action that signals value. If users aren't hitting this, the idea is flawed.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a tracking schema for a SaaS MVP focusing on user retention, specifically identifying the activation event where a user reaches their 'Aha!' moment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Create a schema.prisma file that includes an events table for tracking user interaction timestamps and metadata.'

steps.title: Instrument the User Journey
steps.description: Inject tracking at key friction points like signup, onboarding completion, and the core utility feature.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable React hook that wraps click events with metadata, automatically sending them to an analytics endpoint.

steps.title: Deploy the Analytics Backend
steps.description: Set up a lightweight ingestion point that doesn't bloat your main application performance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a serverless API route to ingest JSON analytics payloads and store them in a PostgreSQL database with indexed timestamps.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Implement a Next.js API route in /api/ingest that validates incoming analytics events.'

steps.title: Visualize the Friction
steps.description: Convert raw logs into a funnel visualization to see exactly where users drop off.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a SQL query to calculate conversion rates between the 'signup' event and the 'core_feature_used' event.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Analyze the database and output a summary of user drop-off points between signup and feature usage.'

steps.title: Iterate Based on Data
steps.description: Use the drop-off data to pivot your feature set. If no one uses the feature, kill it.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a report summarizing potential feature pivots based on low engagement metrics in the provided analytics logs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Review existing logs and suggest three high-value feature adjustments based on current user behavior.'

pro_tips: Never track everything; track only the events that prove your core hypothesis.
Implement 'identity resolution' early so you can track the same user across multiple devices.
Correlate analytics events with churn logs to see exactly what pushed a user to leave.
Use session recording alongside event tracking to see the 'why' behind the 'what'.
Automate your analytics reporting to Slack so you see real-time signals without logging into a dashboard.
content.next_steps: Analyze your first 100 users, identify the friction points, and ship an update that directly addresses the most common drop-off reason.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start engineering for growth. Our community of high-output founders is waiting to help you scale your MVP. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
